issues.message: Inconsistent verbs are used to create secondary objects. Sinks use 'create-sink' whereas schedules use 'add-schedule' (should be 'create-schedule').
Inconsistent verbs are used to delete secondary objects. Sinks use 'delete-sink' whereas schedules use 'remove-schedule' (should be 'delete-schedule').
The show command for todo is renamed to 'info' (info <todo-id>), which uses a noun instead of a verb, violating the standard requirement that commands acting on a primary object must be verbs.
Listing commands ('sinks', 'schedules') output JSON formatting by default instead of a tabular structure when '--format table' is specified or defaulted.
Table column headers printed by 'todo list' are not rendered in bold font.
Table column headers cannot be hidden via a '--no-headers' flag.
Daemon status and stop commands use 'failed' error messages instead of 'cannot'.

The sink detail command is renamed to 'show-sink' (show-sink <sink-id>) instead of 'sink <sink-id>', violating the standard shorthand pattern where secondary objects use 'foobar <id>' instead of 'show-foobar'.
Default exact date/time formatting (without --rfc3339) uses non-ISO-8601 format (2006-01-02 15:04 MST).
